memoryArtifacts.headline: James Tomlinson - Ancestry of Larry Joe Hiatt
memoryArtifacts.description:
Born in 1801, in Fayette County, Kentucky; the son of William Tomlinson.

At the age of eleven, James moved with his family to Maury County, Tennessee, settling in the Culleoka area.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Page於1605年5月1日出生在英國諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 邁克爾時，他的父親Richard Robert Page40歲，母親Susanna Syckerlyn39歲。他於1619年5月28日與Francisca Atkinson在英國越可爾吉斯本結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1676年3月10日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可拉帕韓諾可享年70歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州笛卡爾 拉帕韓諾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Page于1605年5月1日出生在英国諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 邁克爾时，他的父亲Richard Robert Page40岁，母亲Susanna Syckerlyn39岁。他于1619年5月28日与Francisca Atkinson在英国越可爾吉斯本结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1676年3月10日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可拉帕韓諾可享年70岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州笛卡爾 拉帕韓諾可。

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Page was born on 1 May 1605, in Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England, his father, Richard Robert Page, was 40 and his mother, Susanna Syckerlyn, was 39. He married Francisca Atkinson on 28 May 1619, in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 10 March 1676, in Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Page est né le 1 mai 1605, à Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Richard Robert Page, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Susanna Syckerlyn, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Francisca Atkinson le 28 mai 1619, à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 10 mars 1676, à Rappahannock, Essex, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Old Rappahannock, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Page am 1. Mai 1605 in Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Robert Page 40 und seine Mutter Susanna Syckerlyn 39. Er hat am 28. Mai 1619 in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England Francisca Atkinson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 10. März 1676 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America und wurde in Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Page è nato il il 1 maggio 1605 nel Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Richard Robert Page, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Susanna Syckerlyn, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Francisca Atkinson il 28 maggio 1619, nel Gisburn, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 10 marzo 1676, nel Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Pageが1605年5月1日にイギリスノーフォルクオルメスビー スト ミチャエルで生まれた時，父Richard Robert Pageは40歳，母Susanna Syckerlynは39歳であった。1619年5月28日にイギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンでFrancisca Atkinsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1676年3月10日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州エセックスラッパハノックで70歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州オルド ラッパハノックにて埋葬された。

ko.Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스 랩아해노c크
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Page(이)가 1605년 5월 1일 영국 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 믹하얼에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Robert Page(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Susanna Syckerlyn(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1619년 5월 28일 영국 요르크실르 지스번에서 Francisca Atkinson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1676년 3월 10일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스 랩아해노c크에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 드 랩아아노크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Page nasceu em 1 de maio de 1605, em Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Robert Page, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Susanna Syckerlyn, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Francisca Atkinson em 28 de maio de 1619, em Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de março de 1676, em Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Page родился 1 мая 1605 г. в местности ормесби ст мачаел, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Robert Page было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Susanna Syckerlyn, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Francisca Atkinson 28 мая 1619 г. в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 10 марта 1676 г. в местности раппаханнокк, эссекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности олд раппаханнокк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Page nació el 1 de mayo de 1605, en Ormesby St Michael, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Robert Page, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Susanna Syckerlyn, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Francisca Atkinson el 28 de mayo de 1619, en Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 10 de marzo de 1676, en Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Old Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial America.
